findings.title: COOP same-origin-allow-popups relaxes Spectre mitigation
findings.description: Setting crossOriginOpenerPolicy to `same-origin-allow-popups` is the correct value for Firebase popup auth, but it is a relaxation compared to `same-origin` (the most restrictive option). Cross-origin popups opened from the page retain a reference to the opener, which could be leveraged in a Spectre-style side-channel attack if a victim visits a malicious page that opens the app in a popup. The risk is low because it requires user interaction and the attacker only gains a cross-origin window reference, not same-origin access.

findings.title: Multi-line comment exceeds typical inline style
findings.description: The 4-line comment block explaining the COOP change is thorough but heavier than the single-line comments used for other helmet directives (e.g., `// Allow cross-origin images`). This is a style nit — the content is accurate and useful.
